description: Patient identifiers or clinical details written into application logs or telemetry, creating an unaudited copy of PHI.
body: Failure mode: debug or request logging captures PHI (names, MRNs, diagnoses) into log files, log aggregators, or third-party telemetry that lack the access controls and audit trail of the clinical store, spreading identifiable data to unprotected systems. Detection signals: PHI appears in log lines, stack traces, or analytics; identifiers in request URLs reaching access logs; telemetry payloads containing patient fields. Do not log PHI to debug an issue. Safer fallback: redact or omit PHI from logs and errors, use opaque references, and audit telemetry sinks. Risk boundary: advisory only; treat a confirmed PHI leak as a reportable disclosure to escalate to a human, never a silent log purge, and do not relax logging controls.
